task_completion.description: Verify that the observer location is set to Paranal Observatory, Chile, or its exact coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a location settings window or an on-screen status bar indicating 'Paranal Observatory', 'Chile', or coordinates matching 24.6272° S, 70.4042° W (or ~24°37'S, 70°24'W) and altitude 2635m.

task_completion.description: Verify that the date is set to December 21, 2020, and the time is approximately 18:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a date/time control window or status bar showing the date '2020-12-21' and time around '18:00' (or the correct local time equivalent if a UTC offset is displayed).

task_completion.description: Verify that Jupiter and Saturn are located in the sky view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the visual presence of Jupiter and Saturn in the sky map, typically indicated by text labels for 'Jupiter' and 'Saturn'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the view is centered on the Jupiter and Saturn conjunction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jupiter and Saturn should be positioned near the center of the application's viewport or crosshairs.

task_completion.description: Verify that the field of view is zoomed in enough so that both planets and their moons are clearly visible in a single view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a highly magnified view where the planetary disks of Jupiter and Saturn are visible, and their respective moons (e.g., Io, Europa, Titan) are visible as distinct dots or have visible text labels, all within the same screen.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface of an astronomy application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a genuine planetarium or astronomy software interface (like Stellarium) with menus, toolbars, and a rendered sky, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the sky view is actively rendered by the software and not a static image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for active UI overlays, selection markers, or FOV (Field of View) indicators interacting with the sky view, proving it is not just a loaded image in a photo viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the target parameters into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editors (e.g., Notepad, TextEdit) or terminal windows simply displaying the text 'Paranal Observatory', '2020-12-21', or 'Jupiter and Saturn' as a shortcut.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Great Conjunction of Jupiter and Saturn occurred on 2020-12-21 in the constellation of Capricornus with a separation of approximately 6.1 arcminutes. The target location is Paranal Observatory (Latitude: -24.6272, Longitude: -70.4042). Jupiter's magnitude was approximately -1.977.
